steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence introduces "the novelist" without naming anyone yet, so the name "Stephen Graham Jones" is essential to identify which novelist is meant. An essential (restrictive) appositive takes no punctuation separating it from its noun.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'novelist Stephen Graham Jones': no punctuation between noun and name — treats the name as essential and identifying. ✓
  • Choice B — 'novelist, Stephen Graham Jones,': commas on both sides mark the name as non-essential, implying the reader already knows which novelist is meant. No such context exists. ✗
  • Choice C — 'novelist. Stephen Graham Jones': the period leaves "the editors turned to the novelist" incomplete and makes the name a standalone fragment. ✗
  • Choice D — 'novelist Stephen Graham Jones,': the trailing comma incorrectly separates the full subject from its verb, breaking the sentence. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
